headline: Aladdin Wealth launches AI Auto Commentary tool; Morgan Stanley Portfolio Risk Platform first to deploy
the_so_what: BlackRock embedding generative AI directly into Aladdin Wealth — with Morgan Stanley as the first deployer — turns AI-generated portfolio narratives into a platform-level feature rather than a build-it-yourself project, following the same pattern as Bloomberg embedding analytics into terminals: once the infrastructure vendor ships it, the capability becomes an industry default.
summary: Aladdin Wealth, BlackRock's wealth management technology platform, has launched an AI-powered 'Auto Commentary' feature that converts complex portfolio analytics and client investment preferences into concise, personalized narratives for financial advisors. The tool combines Aladdin Wealth's risk analytics, a firm's CIO market outlook, and client portfolio data, using generative AI to assess hundreds of data points and surface the most relevant insights. Morgan Stanley's Portfolio Risk Platform — which integrates BlackRock's Aladdin risk analytics with Morgan Stanley data — will be the first to implement Auto Commentary, beginning in October 2025. The feature is designed to reduce time spent on labor-intensive research so advisors can focus more on client conversations and delivering actionable insights.

verified_claims: Aladdin Wealth is BlackRock's wealth management technology platform
The new feature is called 'Auto Commentary'
Auto Commentary converts complex portfolio analytics and client investment preferences into concise, personalized narratives for financial advisors
The tool combines Aladdin Wealth's risk analytics, a firm's CIO market outlook, and client portfolio data
It uses generative AI to assess hundreds of data points and surface the most relevant insights
Morgan Stanley's Portfolio Risk Platform integrates BlackRock's Aladdin risk analytics with Morgan Stanley data

Implementation begins in October (2025 implied from context)

unverified_claims: The generated entry states the implementation begins 'in October 2025' — the source says 'Starting in October' but does not explicitly state the year 2025 in that sentence (though the press release date implies it)
_governance.notes: Nearly all claims in the generated entry are well-supported by the source article.
